source: Learning Rsync - inotify-tools installation and use: Compiling inotify-tools and adjusting the inotify kernel parameters

questions.q: Which two commands does inotify-tools provide?
What is the difference between inotifywait and inotifywatch?
options: inotifywait monitors continuously with real-time output; inotifywatch monitors short-term and reports after finishing
questions.why: inotifywait streams events live (used with rsync); inotifywatch summarizes after the task.

questions.q: Why does the author compile inotify-tools from source rather than use the EPEL RPM?
options: For efficiency and a newer version (bug fixes/features, possibly higher transfer efficiency) in production
questions.why: It is the author's personal recommendation: a newer, potentially more efficient build for production.

questions.q: After installing to a custom prefix, how do you put the binaries permanently on PATH?
options: Append PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/inotify-tools/bin/ in /etc/profile and re-source it
Copy them into /bin by hand each boot
questions.why: Write the PATH addition into /etc/profile, then . /etc/profile.
queue size, number of instances, and files watched per instance.

questions.q: Apply kernel parameters you have written into /etc/sysctl.conf.
